oneLineDescription: Evaluate entry into the developer observability market targeting mid-market SaaS companies underserved by enterprise tools
goal: Validate product-market fit with 15 customer interviews and a go/no-go recommendation by 30 April 2026
scope: Developer observability for mid-market SaaS (200-2000 employees). Focus on APM, log management, and distributed tracing
outOfScope: Infrastructure monitoring, security observability, enterprise (>2000 employees), SMB (<200 employees)
strategicHypothesis: Mid-market SaaS companies will pay $15-25k/year for an observability platform that offers 80% of enterprise features at 40% of the cost, with a self-serve onboarding experience that doesn't require a dedicated SRE team
icpDescription: VP Engineering or Head of Platform at a SaaS company with 200-2000 employees, currently using a mix of open-source tools and feeling the pain of fragmented observability
killConditions: Fewer than 4/15 prospects rank observability fragmentation in their top 3 pain points
No WTP signal above $12k/year after 10 interviews
3+ prospects say they'd build in-house rather than buy
Datadog launches a mid-market tier before our target launch date
